supporting_works.title: The elements of materia medica and therapeutics / by Jonathan Pereira.

Modern domestic medicine: : a popular treatise, illustrating the symptoms, causes, distinction, and correct treatment of the diseases incident to the human frame; embracing the modern improvements in medicine. To which are added, a domestic materia medica; a copious collection of approved prescriptions, &c. &c. retrieval_text: Guaiacum is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 327 works and 843 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Guaiacum, Guaiacum Gum. Common plant-part co-mentions include Resin, Gum, Root, Bark, Seed, Leaf. Common preparation co-mentions include Tincture, Powder, Decoction, Pill, Oil, Tea. Common condition co-mentions include Rheumatism, Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Gout, Fever, Vomiting or nausea. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Toxicity watch. Representative source works include The elements of materia medica and therapeutics / by Jonathan Pereira.; Dr. Pereira's Elements of materia medica and therapeutics : abridged and adapted for the use of medicine and pharmaceutical practitioners and students and comprising all of the medicines of the British Pharmacopœia, with such others as are frequently ordered in prescriptions or required by the physician / edited by Robert Bently and Theophilus Redwood.; Dr. Pereira's Elements of materia medica and therapeutics : abridged and adapted for the use of medical and pharmaceutical practitioners and students and comprising all the medicines of the British Pharmacopœia, with such others as are frequently ordered in prescriptions or required by the physician / edited by Robert Bentley and Theophilus Redwood ; with an appendix.; The American eclectic dispensatory. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
